summary: I was born and raised in Japan, specifically in Tokyo, by my single mother who always encouraged me to explore the world. Growing up in Japan, I had a curious spirit and a desire to challenge myself beyond my comfort zone. My experiences in Japan laid the foundation for my journey abroad and shaped my perspective on life and opportunities.

Tokyo is where I grew up and where my story began. Living in this bustling city, I was raised by my single mother who took me on many trips to explore the world. It was from Tokyo that I made the bold decision at age 16 to leave high school and study abroad in Canada, a choice that drastically changed my life.

Canada became a pivotal place in my life when I moved there at 16 to study abroad on a government scholarship. Being chosen as the sole Japanese youth representative among thousands was both an honor and a heavy pressure. In Canada, I struggled with balancing expectations and happiness, but eventually found joy through new friendships, diverse communities, and activities like volleyball and fundraising, which transformed my outlook on life.

British Columbia was the province in Canada where I studied as an international student. Out of 12,500 Japanese students there, I was honored to be selected as the representative through a merit-based scholarship. Living and working hard in British Columbia taught me resilience, but also made me realize the importance of happiness and community beyond just academic success.
